title: Sideroxylon chartaceum
taxonomy: Sideroxylon chartaceum is classified within the plant family Sapotaceae. This species belongs to the genus Sideroxylon. The classification of Sideroxylon chartaceum can be summarized as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Ericales, Family: Sapotaceae, Genus: Sideroxylon, Species: S. chartaceum. Further classification may vary based on different taxonomic sources.

benefits: Sideroxylon chartaceum, also known as the paperbark tree, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Sideroxylon chartaceum, also known as the chartaceous bullock, is a plant species belonging to the Ebenaceae family, characterized by its unique and striking appearance. Traditionally, the chartaceous bullock has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Sideroxylon chartaceum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the chartaceous bullock is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts, to harness its potential health benefits.
